channel.title: Yes, You Can Dance!
channel.description: Special Needs, Senior & MS Dancing NonProfit | Jupiter, FL

item.title: Page Archives
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded:
This is an example page. It's different from a blog post because it will stay in one place and will show up in your site navigation (in most themes). Most people start with an About page that introduces them to potential site visitors. It might say something like this:
Hi there! I'm a bike messenger by day, aspiring actor by night, and this is my website. I live in Los Angeles, have a great dog named Jack, and I like piña coladas. (And gettin' caught in the rain.)
...or something like this:
The XYZ Doohickey Company was founded in 1971, and has been providing quality doohickeys to the public ever since. Located in Gotham City, XYZ employs over 2,000 people and does all kinds of awesome things for the Gotham community.
As a new WordPress user, you should go to your dashboard to delete this page and create new pages for your content. Have fun!

item.title: Home
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded:
Yes, You Can Dance! is a non-profit organization dedicated to transforming lives through the power of dance.  We partner with community organizations, dance professionals, health professionals and educators to design and provide dance opportunities for individuals with special needs, people with Multiple Sclerosis, and more.

The benefits are unmatched when it comes to being part of our mission, as we strive to provide growth in areas of patience, compassion and living in the moment. Not only is dance great for physical health, but it also plays a large role in both mental and emotional health as well. Our curriculums bring about positive changes to emotions and attitudes through dance, as an instantaneous outlet for stress relief and enhanced motor skills.

Joining Yes, You Can Dance! improves overall self-esteem and attentiveness in most of our individuals, as dance therapy is known to increase confidence, social and communication skills.

We want you to be imaginative and creative, and that's exactly what the power of dance allows you to do.

item.title: Contact Us
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded:
Please contact us using the form below for:
  • Media / Press inquiries
  • To learn more about our programs, licensing and certification
  • Grant information
Find a location with Yes, You Can Dance! programs. [everest_form id="7641"]

item.title: OLD: Who Dances?
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded:
Seniors Dance!

Click here to learn more about our Senior Social program.

Adults and Teens with Special Needs Dance!

Click here to learn more about our Special Needs Ballroom program.

Those with Multiple Sclerosis Dance!

Click here to learn more about our Special Needs Ballroom program.

Volunteers Dance!

Click here to learn more about our volunteers.

You Dance!

Click here to learn about how you can get involved with Yes, You Can Dance! (whether you dance or not!)

item.title: Our Team
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded:
Yes, You Can Dance! is supported by a dedicated and talented team.  Together, they bring many years of professional expertise and unique talents to helping Yes, You Can Dance! fulfill its mission and reach its highest potential. Find out more about each of these amazing individuals below.

Deborah Rosen Fidel

Executive Director Deborah Rosen Fidel has been serving the public sector and non-profit community for the last 24 years in both a volunteer and professional capacity.  Deborah began her career as an attorney in private practice, but recognized early on that she wanted  to use her training and skills to improve the quality of life for others.  She brings considerable experience and successes in fundraising, marketing, program oversight, volunteer management honed throughout her career to her new role as the Executive Director of  Yes , You Can Dance!, but  it is her passion for people and for creating opportunities for everyone to experience joy through dance that most excites her about this opportunity.    Deborah earned a B.A. from Brandeis University, a J.D. from Boston University School of Law and an M.J.P.S. from the Spertus Institute.  She and her husband live in Squirrel Hill and are the parents of three sons and a dog. Contact Deborah

Chris Roth

Director of Curriculum and Instruction Chris is a gifted and award winning competitive ballroom dancer and teacher.  He has 24 years of professional experience and is a certified GOLD instructor in American Ballroom and Rhythm.  He boasts eleven Pro/Am top teacher awards including Top Teacher of the United States Ballroom Championships in the U.S. Open of Dance. Chris is the original instructor for Yes, You Can Dance! Special Needs Ballroom Program and the creative genius behind its curriculum.   His enthusiasm and natural gifts as a teacher are fundamental to the success of our programs. Chris is also the owner and director of his own successful ballroom dance studio in Pittsburgh, Steel City Ballroom. Contact Chris

Lauren Stash-Sisco

Program and Administrative Coordinator  Lauren has worked for Joy of Motion Dance Center, The National Museum of Women in the Arts, The Heinz History Center, Giant Eagle, and danced professionally with Dakshina Dance Company in Washington, DC. She likes to say that she was a dancer in a “previous life,” and is thrilled to be marrying her previous life and her current life at Yes, You Can Dance!.

Lauren is a Pittsburgh native and earned her degree in dance from The George Washington University in Washington, DC. She lives in South Oakland with her husband and chatty 3-year-old son.

Contact Lauren

Elise Pure

Special Needs Ballroom Program Instructor and Studio Assistant Elise has had a passion for dance since her parents enrolled her in Ballet classes at the age of three. For fifteen years she studied Ballet and additionally trained in Tap, Jazz, Irish Step, and Contemporary dance styles before leaving for college. Her love for ballroom dance began after joining Salsa and Swing dance clubs at Messiah College where she graduated with a B.A. in Psychology and a minor in Dance. Inspired by the therapeutic potential of dance, she began a dance program at a YMCA near her hometown where she taught various dance classes for two years. Currently she is pursuing her Master's degree in Occupational Therapy at the University of Pittsburgh. Elise is ecstatic to be involved in Yes, You Can Dance! and work with their amazing team of talented individuals to share her love of dance with others.  

Emily Fitchwell

Special Needs Ballroom Program Instructor & Partners in PE Special Needs Ballroom Instructor  Emily graduated from Slippery Rock University with a B.S. degree in Health and Physical Education and earned her Master’s degree in Exercise Science with a specialization in Health and Wellness at California University of PA. She teaches Health, Physical Education, Partners in PE, and Partners Aquatics at Belle Vernon Area High School and also enjoys coaching track and field each spring. Emily heard about Yes, You Can Dance! in 2015 and immediately took advantage of the opportunity to learn this unique, inclusive based curriculum. She began ballroom dancing in the summer of 2015. In early spring of 2016, she modeled the program with her Partners in PE students with great success! Partners in Physical Education is a course that is growing in various school districts across the country that unites students in the regular education classroom to partner, mentor, coach and develop strong friendships with students who have special needs. Emily’s mission is to educate and inspire all individuals about the vital importance of maintaining a healthy, active lifestyle with the ultimate goal of improving ones quality of life and is honored to become a member of the Yes, You Can Dance! team.  

Molly Levine

Special Needs Ballroom Program Studio Assistant Molly is a Pittsburgh native and Penn State graduate with experience in the hospitality and non-profit sectors. She took ballet and jazz lessons from ages 3-15 and now participates in Zumba classes. Molly is thrilled to bring her passion for connecting with others and her administrative skills to the North Hills Yes, You Can Dance! location and is looking forward to learning some new dances moves along the way!  

Lauriellen Bochner

Special Needs Ballroom Program Studio Assistant Lauriellen graduated from Indiana University of Pennsylvania with a degree in English Literature. She has spent more than 30 years dancing and performing in Pittsburgh and Washington, DC. While most of her training is in ballet, she is also trained in modern and jazz. In her career and as part of the community, Lauriellen has worked with individuals with special needs, including coaching figure skaters for Special Olympics, dance instruction and equine (riding) therapy. Lauriellen is also an avid equestrian and is currently training in dressage.

item.title: About Us
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded:
Our Mission

Dancing is the hidden language of the soul, and we’re committed to enhancing the  physical and emotional well-being of individuals with intellectual, developmental, and/or physical disabilities through social dance experiences.

Yes, You Can Dance! has partnered with dance professionals, educators, and healthcare providers to develop curriculums that provide innovative and fun-filled experiences for people with a variety of disabilities.  Participants learn to ballroom dance in a safe and supportive atmosphere.

Our Organization

Yes, You Can Dance! was founded by Rebecca Stern in 2011, in order to share the wellness benefits and the joy of ballroom dancing with people who might otherwise not have the opportunity,  due to an intellectual, developmental, and/or physical disability.

Our team of  dance professionals, specialists in behavioral health, healthcare, and special educators create innovative and fun-filled experiences for people with a variety of disabilities to learn ballroom dance in a safe and supportive atmosphere.  Trained Volunteer Dance Partners pair up with our dance students for encouragement and support.

Our Founder

Rebecca Stern, Founder and President

Rebecca is professionally trained to be an educator focusing on practical life experiences for students through Special Education, Business Education, and Instructional Technology.  In the fall of 2007, Rebecca was elected to Upper St. Clair School District School Board.

She began Ballroom Dancing in March 2008.  By October, she had entered her first Dance competition in Miami, Florida.  Since then she has competed in American Rhythm and Smooth categories.  In addition, she enjoys Argentine Tango and West Coast Swing.

In her own words: “Learning to Ballroom Dance has been transformative for me. It is a great form of physical exercise for me. I have joined a worldwide community of dancers.  Wherever I travel, I can find a dance event. I have gained a new confidence in myself.”

item.title: Website Information
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded:
Thank you for visiting Yes You Can Dance. All text, video, and photos copyright Yes You Can Dance, unless otherwise noted. Please contact us for more information on use, or for a Press Kit. If you see problems with this site, please let us know. This site takes advantage of CSS3 and is best viewed in modern browsers. If you are using an old web browser, please consider upgrading to the latest version for best viewing experience. We recommend Firefox, Safari or Chrome.

Website Credits:

Website Designed and Developed by Project 18, Inc. and Dabbled Studios, in Atlanta GA. Making the world a little more beautiful and usable, one website at a time. www.project18.com www.dabbledstudios.com   The lovely font used on this site for headings: Dancing Script by Impallari

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded: What information do we collect? We collect information from you when you register on our site, subscribe to our newsletter, respond to a survey, fill out a form or sign up for an event, make a donation. When ordering or registering on our site, as appropriate, you may be asked to enter your: name, e-mail address, mailing address, phone number or credit card information. You may, however, visit our site anonymously. What do we use your information for? Any of the information we collect from you may be used in one of the following ways: ; To improve customer service (your information helps us to more effectively respond to your customer service requests and support needs) ; To process transactions Your information, whether public or private, will not be sold, exchanged, transferred, or given to any other company for any reason whatsoever, without your consent, other than for the express purpose of delivering the purchased product or service requested. ; To administer a contest, promotion, survey or other site feature ; To send periodic emails The email address you provide for order processing, may be used to send you information and updates pertaining to your order, in addition to receiving occasional company news, updates, related product or service information, etc. Note: If at any time you would like to unsubscribe from receiving future emails, we include detailed unsubscribe instructions at the bottom of each email. How do we protect your information? We implement a variety of security measures to maintain the safety of your personal information when you place an order or enter, submit, or access your personal information. We offer the use of a secure server. All supplied sensitive/credit information is transmitted via Secure Socket Layer (SSL) technology and then encrypted into our Payment gateway providers database only to be accessible by those authorized with special access rights to such systems, and are required to?keep the information confidential. After a transaction, your private information (credit cards, social security numbers, financials, etc.) will not be kept on file for more than 60 days. Do we use cookies? We do not use cookies. Do we disclose any information to outside parties? We do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer to outside parties your personally identifiable information. This does not include trusted third parties who assist us in operating our website, conducting our business, or servicing you, so long as those parties agree to keep this information confidential. We may also release your information when we believe release is appropriate to comply with the law, enforce our site policies, or protect ours or others rights, property, or safety. However, non-personally identifiable visitor information may be provided to other parties for marketing, advertising, or other uses. Third party links Occasionally, at our discretion, we may include or offer third party products or services on our website. These third party sites have separate and independent privacy policies. We therefore have no responsibility or liability for the content and activities of these linked sites. Nonetheless, we seek to protect the integrity of our site and welcome any feedback about these sites. California Online Privacy Protection Act Compliance Because we value your privacy we have taken the necessary precautions to be in compliance with the California Online Privacy Protection Act. We therefore will not distribute your personal information to outside parties without your consent. As part of the California Online Privacy Protection Act, all users of our site may make any changes to their information at anytime by logging into their control panel and going to the 'Edit Profile' page. Childrens Online Privacy Protection Act Compliance We are in compliance with the requirements of COPPA (Childrens Online Privacy Protection Act), we do not collect any information from anyone under 13 years of age. Our website, products and services are all directed to people who are at least 13 years old or older. Terms and Conditions Please also visit our Terms and Conditions section establishing the use, disclaimers, and limitations of liability governing the use of our website at https://www.yesyoucandance.org Your Consent By using our site, you consent to our privacy policy. Changes to our Privacy Policy If we decide to change our privacy policy, we will update the Privacy Policy modification date below. This policy was last modified on March 1, 2012 Contacting Us If there are any questions regarding this privacy policy you may contact us using the information below. www.yesyoucandance.org 1540 Fox Chase Lane Pittsburgh, PA 15241 United States info@yesyoucandance.org This policy is powered by Free Privacy Policy and Rhino Support help desk software.

item.title: Step 2: Payment
item.dc:creator: Karen
item.content:encoded:
Payment can be made online by Credit Card or Paypal, or you may send a check by regular mail.  You will be receiving a copy of your Donation Form via email. Please contact us if any corrections are needed. Payment Option 1: Online Donation: To make your donation online, either by Credit Card or Paypal, please click the button below to complete your transaction at Paypal.
  Payment Option 2: Donate by Check If you wish to Pay by Check, please mail your check to Yes You Can Dance! Attn: Donations 1540 Fox Chase Lane Pittsburgh PA 15241 Please also print and include your Donation form, or include your email address on your check. Thank you for supporting Yes You Can Dance!

item.title: High Schoolers
item.dc:creator: Karen
item.content:encoded:
Yes, You Can Dance! gives high schools and high schoolers the chance to create lifelong connections while learning at the same time. Our In-School Dance Curriculum provides an educational opportunity for both special needs and those without special needs to come together and become dancers.

If you're an educator searching for a unique opportunity to provide your class with something outside of the box, consider Yes, You Can Dance! Your students will receive the opportunity to challenge themselves to work with each other as well as special needs students to create an unforgettable experience. Our curriculum meets Physical Education requirements for most high schools, ensuring that students receive credits for participating in the program.

Aside from this, your students will grow as individuals while learning the art of ballroom dancing.

To learn more about our In-School Dance Curriculum, click here.

item.title: Make A Donation
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded: Our mission wouldn’t be possible without our supporters. Your donations allow Yes, You Can Dance! to continually deliver dance-based therapy and curriculums to adults and students who learn expression and interaction through movement. Aside from a tax-deductible donation, other ways to give include United Way contributions, AmazonSmile donations, and corporate matching when applicable. As a 501(c)(3) non-profit, you can view our Designation Letter here. With your support, we are able to offer comprehensive ballroom dance curriculums for individuals and organizations to capitalize on, bring high-quality dance instructions to people with special needs, MS, and more, facilitate the growth of Yes, You Can Dance! programs in other communities around the country and share the joys and benefits of dance with people of all abilities and ages! [give_form id="6256"]

item.title: What Is Yes, You Can Dance!?
item.dc:creator: Becky
item.content:encoded:
Our Mission

Yes, You Can Dance! enhances the physical and emotional well being of individuals and communities through social dance experiences.

Our Organization

Yes, You Can Dance! was founded by Rebecca Stern in 2011, with the mission of using dance to support wellness for those with special needs, disabilities, and chronic degenerative diseases. We join dance professionals with specialists in behavioral health, healthcare, and special education in order to design appropriate curriculum for a variety of different programs. Our team of highly trained dance instructors teach a curriculum designed for each specific population, in ongoing consultation with a specialist knowledgeable about that population’s therapeutic needs. Program participants are paired with volunteer  “dance mentors” or "dance hosts" who function as dance partners throughout the curriculum under the supervision of the instructor. Yes, You Can Dance! began operations with its Special Needs Ballroom Program designed for adults and teens with intellectual challenges and/or disabilities. We also serve seniors through the Senior Social Program where professional dance instructors and volunteer dance hosts work with seniors at varying levels of physical activity.

item.title: Multiple Sclerosis
item.dc:creator: Becky
item.content:encoded:
Yes, You Can Dance! is proud to offer the MS Ballroom Program through a partnership with the University of Pittsburgh's Physical Therapy Department in the School of Health and Rehabilitation Sciences and the MS Society of Western Pennsylvania.

Those with a confirmed diagnosis of Relapsing-Remitting Multiple Sclerosis are eligible to participate. No previous dance experience is necessary.  Each participant is paired with a trained Volunteer Dance Partner, who provides individual attention and support to his/her partner as they learn and practice various ballroom dances.  You may also attend with your own partner if you prefer.

More than just a fun activity or art form, dance is gaining attention as a health benefit in several different ways. We know that the brain exhibits plastic changes throughout life, rewiring its neural pathways in response to challenging situations. Activities such as partner dancing, which require split-second decision making, and the need to perform directional changes on a regular basis routinely force the brain to respond to new and challenging stimuli. A study published in the New England Journal of Medicine in 2003 (J Verghese) reports that dance is associated with a reduced risk of dementia—the only physical activity out of those studied to do so. While physical activity is important for everyone, particularly for those with chronic disease, not everyone can run a marathon. It is imperative, however, that people find a physical activity in which they can participate and enjoy doing…"enjoy" being the key. Dance not only provides a form of physical exercise, but also a fun, social experience, and we believe, is a stimulus for positive neuroplastic changes capable of improving both physical and cognitive functioning, while at the same time improving quality of life. Our hope is that people with MS who participate in our dance program will gain improvements in both physical functioning and quality of life, and ultimately, have the to confidence and ability to dance at family parties and social dance events in the Pittsburgh community.

The eight-week sessions are held weekly for one hour on Saturdays at noon at the Pitt School of  Health and Rehabilitation Sciences.  MS Ballroom classes sessions are offered twice a year.

Try it out!  MS Ballroom students pay a mere $60.00, thanks to The MS Society of Western Pennsylvania, who generously underwrites half of each student's $120 tuition.   If you decide that the MS Ballroom Program is not for you after the first class, you can get a $50 refund on your $60 tuition.  After the second class, you can get a $40 refund.

If you are interested in learning more or joining our next session, click here or email us at info@yesyoucandance.org. Volunteers, please email or call 412-341-1286 and we will place you on the roster.

Click here to learn more!

item.title: Home-How Can I Help
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded:
[testimonial_view id="1"]

Open YOUR heart and join us today!

[give_form id="4341"]

item.title: Licensing
item.dc:creator: Karen
item.content:encoded:
Yes, You Can Dance! believes dance creates a powerful connection through nonverbal communication that looks past any disabilities and instead focuses on the joys of dancing. Thanks to our comprehensive curriculums and complete training resources, you can bring a sense of community to your organization, program or school. These carefully-designed curriculums are customized to meet the needs of those with cognitive disabilities. For individuals with Multiple Sclerosis, curriculums are designed with physical limitations in mind, focusing on slower tempo movements. Our training programs are aimed at helping dance teachers get licensed in order to help launch and sustain exceptional programs that cater to those with special needs. Yes, You Can Dance! wants to ensure other dance studios, school districts or other similar programs have the opportunity to take advantage of an amazing resource in their local communities. When you partner with Yes, You Can Dance!, you will receive individualized training to use for your program.

How to Get Certified

For Individuals If you’re a dance instructor or individual looking to become an expert in your field, Yes, You Can Dance! gives you the perfect opportunity to allow for organizers and professionals to incorporate special needs dance into their programs and curriculums. Experience the positive experience in becoming a certified Yes,You Can Dance! instructor. In order to learn more about our individual opportunities, click here. For Organizations Is your organization interested in developing a community of dancers of varied abilities? The Yes, You Can Dance! model will do just that. We encourage educational institutions and other organizations with a shared mission to learn more about our curriculums and explore our ballroom dance training guides. Our hope is that schools and other programs alike will find inspiration in this program to facilitate change through the power of dance. If this sounds like the right fit for your program, contact us to learn more. If you're ready to get started today, contact us.

[button link="https://www.yesyoucandance.org/contact-us/"]Get Started Today![/button]

item.title: Donation Confirmation
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin

item.content:encoded: We're sorry, your transaction failed to process. Please try again or contact site support.

item.title: Donation History
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded: [donation_history]

item.title: Curriculums
item.dc:creator: Karen
item.content:encoded: Yes, You Can Dance! is thrilled to offer two all-inclusive curriculums for Certified Instructors and for In-School Dance Programs and Organizations. Both programs reflect the mission of our program and provide instruction for prosperous programs to touch the lives of everyone involved – from individuals to families to instructors alike. Special Needs Ballroom Curriculum for Certified Instructors  If you're an individual who wants to play a part in transforming lives through joy and the power of dance, Yes, You Can Dance! has an individual curriculum for you. As a certified instructor, you are the key to the quality of the program and have a direct impact on the beneficial outcomes for your students. This curriculum walks you through everything from how to modify your ballroom to step-by-step instructions for each dance move. To see a sample of our special needs curriculum, click here. Special Needs Ballroom Curriculum for In-School Dance Program Our In-School Program aims to provide a resource for teachers offering dance within the school environment. This specific curriculum offers lesson plans, training for teachers and equipment to support high schools looking to incorporate ballroom dancing into their lesson plans for individuals with and without special needs. Yes, You Can Dance! offers a variety of lesson plans that meet various education standards. To see a sample lesson of this curriculum, click here. Learn more about our In-School Program.

item.title: Search
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded: Thanks for visiting! If you can't find what you're looking for, please contact us!

item.title: Special Needs
item.dc:creator: Karen
item.content:encoded:
Dance movement is a therapeutic form of exercise which is great not only for physical health but also mental and emotional health.  As a special needs individual, dance possesses the ability to change their lives - even after one session. Yes, You Can Dance! offers dance therapy, which allows one to express emotions through movement that aren't always easily expressed verbally. Even those with limited mobility are still excited to be able to express themselves in their own way through the expression of dance. Aside from this, ballroom dance is an outlet of stress relief, increases physical fitness, encourages creativity and builds self-esteem for those with special needs.

If you're interested in a special needs dance class, find a location here. 

I just want to say thank you for giving my daughter a chance to dance. She is smiling and excited when she comes in each week! You guys are filling a void and doing great things for kids with a desire to dance who happen to be special needs.

Candace, mother of Special Needs Ballroom student

item.title: Press
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded: Yes, You Can Dance is proud to be featured in the news! [testimonials paging=true category="press"]

item.title: Newsletter Archives
item.dc:creator: Karen
item.content:encoded:
To subscribe to our newsletter simply complete the subscription form below.
[navmenu theme_location=newsletr]

item.title: Grants
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded: Grant applications are now welcome! Tell us what you're doing to share the joys of dance. We are accepting grant applications and we want to hear from you. Strong nonprofits create stronger communities, which is why we believe in the power of a grant. Yes, You Can Dance! is excited to announce the beginning of its grant program. We are offering any organization with a similar mission to apply for funding. Our partnerships will focus on specific areas of interest and need in the form of financial support. As long as you share our mission of wanting to transform lives through dance, you're eligible to apply. Our mission is to transform lives through the power of dance.  Since 2011, our focus has been on the needs of adults with Special Needs and the needs of those with Multiple Sclerosis. We recognize that there are many others who would benefit from dance.  Providing funding through grants to programs that target specific needs in their communities is another way for Yes, You Can Dance! to meet our mission. Yes You Can Dance! will be accepting applications annually If you feel your organization aligns with our mission, we want to hear from you. To learn more about our grants program, reach out to our organization. [button link="https://www.yesyoucandance.org/contact-us"]Contact Us[/button]

item.title: Find A Location or Certified Instructor
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded: [wpsl]  Yes, You Can Dance! is committed to expanding our network, and want you to get on board. If you’re a dance studio, school district or other program looking to provide support for those with special needs through dance, we have several locations where you can find licensees to get in contact with. To find a list of licensees in your area, check out the map above To contact a local licensee, use the directory provided to get in touch with a program near you. Can't find a location near you? Please contact us for help and we'll be glad you assist you in the right direction.

item.title: More About Individual Licensing
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded: When you choose to become a certified dance instructor with Yes, You Can Dance!, you immediately become someone who plays a critical role in transforming the lives of others by using your talents. As a certified instructor, your job is to be both knowledgeable and high-spirited to help create lifelong connections using your dance abilities. When you become a leader within our program, it’s your job to earn the trust, respect and admiration of your students and their families. When paired with our curriculum and your energy, instruction and laughter, the results are priceless. You’ll get to share the emotional, physical and social benefits of dance with your students and families, and will also contribute to enhancing the well-being of special needs students and adults who otherwise could have been overlooked. You’ll be able to set the tone and character of your class by treating everyone as equal and will get to witness the benefits and rewards firsthand. Yes, You Can Dance! prides itself on the quality of its instructors, and we view you as a real professional. It’s your job to provide instruction that helps break down stereotypes and barriers to enhance lives. You’re the key to the quality of this program, and we’re so excited you are looking to choose us to help guide you through this journey.

item.title: More About In-School Curriculum
item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
item.content:encoded:
When creating this curriculum, we did it with the hope of introducing students to dance as a form of wellness while partnering special and regular education students in a joint learning environment. The In-School Curriculum is tailored specifically to meet the needs of both students and teachers and was designed with the classroom in mind. This curriculum is an all-encompassing program with lesson plans, teacher training and equipment needed to support high schools interested in using our program to create enjoyable experiences for all involved. All lesson plans build off prior lessons, and both recognize and adapt to meet the needs of all types of learners. Heavy repetition enforces the movements to become a part of students’ muscle memory, which allows all to become successful dancers. Since safety is another concern of ours, we start with base elements of each dance and move forward from there. Once these steps are mastered, trained high school students will be paired with special needs students to learn the different dances as seen in the curriculum.
  • View a sample of our in-school curriculum.
  • Contact us to get started at your school.

item.dc:creator: YYAdmin
